name: Imago Templi
definition: The archetypal Image of the Temple that precedes and governs all earthly temples. Corbin's Latin term for the trans-historical, imaginal reality of the Temple that reveals itself at the 'meeting-place of the two seas' (the barzakh, or mundus imaginalis). It is tautegorical — not a symbol concealing something else, but identical with what it expresses.
